seoTitle: How to Merge Simple Shapes in Canva
metaDescription: Learn how to combine basic shapes into custom merged shapes in Canva for logos, infographics, and branded graphics that stand out.
intro: Merging simple shapes in Canva opens the door to more distinctive logos, infographics, and marketing visuals without needing advanced design software. This tutorial walks through the process of combining shapes and applying it to real design projects.

steps.heading: Access the shapes feature
steps.body: Locate Canva's shape tools where you can select and manipulate basic shapes.

steps.heading: Follow the step-by-step merging process
steps.body: Combine two or more shapes together to form a new, custom design element.

steps.heading: Apply merged shapes to real-world designs
steps.body: Use your merged shapes in projects like logos, infographics, or social media graphics.

steps.heading: Refine your design
steps.body: Adjust colors, sizing, and placement to make the merged shape fit smoothly into your overall design.

faq.question: What can merged shapes be used for?
faq.answer: Merged shapes are commonly used for creating distinctive logos, infographics, and marketing materials.

faq.question: Do I need advanced design skills to merge shapes in Canva?
faq.answer: No, the process is built to be accessible to beginners while still useful for more experienced designers.

faq.question: Can this feature save time when designing multiple graphics?
faq.answer: Yes, merging shapes can streamline your workflow, especially when reused across several related designs.

faq.question: Is this useful for business branding?
faq.answer: Yes, merged shapes are a popular way to create unique visuals for branding and marketing materials.
